name: Kosher for Passover Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 2 Chocolate macaroons, finely ground and suitable for Pesach
2 lb Cream cheese, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Crust: Melt butter and combine with crumbs and sugar in a food processor or with a fork until blended. Press small amounts of the mixture evenly up the sides of an ungreased springform pan, then press the remaining crumbs over the bottom of the springform.
2. Filling: In a mixer, whip cream cheese on high speed for 5 minutes. Add sugar and beat for 2 more minutes. Add lemon juice and salt, and blend thoroughly. Add eggs one at a time, keeping the mixer on the lowest speed until each egg is incorporated. Fold in Brazil nuts with a spatula.
3. If using a food processor: Put sugar first. Cut each 8 oz. block of cream cheese into eight 1-inch cubes and add them to the bowl. Process using an on-off pulse about 25 times, then add the rest of the cream cheese gradually until smooth and creamy. Blend non-stop for 20 seconds more, then blend in lemon juice and salt for 10 seconds. Crack eggs into a bowl, breaking slightly with a fork, and add them to the batter in the processor bowl. Fold lightly with a rubber spatula.
4. Pour batter over crust and bake at 350°F for 55 minutes. Let cool for 10 minutes before glazing.
5. Topping: Combine evenly over the top of the baked filling. Sprinkle nuts around the border, grate lemon zest into the center as decoration, return to oven set at 350°F for 10 minutes. Remove from oven and place immediately in refrigerator to cool, preventing cracks from forming in the cake.
HINT: Most cheesecakes can be made kosher for Passover by replacing any crumbs required with nuts or kosher cookie crumbs. Enjoy!
